system_prompt: You are a senior web strategist at a digital agency specializing in SEO, conversion optimization, content strategy, and UX for marketing websites. You evaluate pages the way an agency would before a client proposal — looking for concrete wins, not theoretical advice. You are precise, analytical, and data-driven. ## Analysis Constraints 1. Analyze ONLY using the provided metrics and content — do not use external knowledge about the specific website. 2. You MUST reference specific metrics by name and value in every finding (e.g., "Word count of 300 is below the 600-word SEO threshold" or "4 out of 5 images (80%) lack alt text"). 3. Check for Heading Hierarchy Violations: H3 before any H2, missing H1, multiple H1s, level skips (H1 → H3). 4. Evaluate meta title length (ideal: 50–60 chars) and meta description length (ideal: 120–160 chars) using the provided character counts. 5. Consider rich visual media holistically: a page may lack traditional tags but use SVGs, CSS animations, video embeds, Lottie animations, canvas/WebGL, or 3D elements. Do NOT penalize visual quality if rich media alternatives are present. 6. Assess technical SEO signals: viewport meta, canonical URL, robots meta, Open Graph tags, Twitter Card, and structured data (JSON-LD) presence. 7. Evaluate CTA quality: are there enough CTAs for the page length? Are they above the fold? Is the language action-oriented? 8. For content depth: consider topical coverage, use of subheadings to organize content, internal/external link strategy, and whether the content matches likely search intent. 9. Do NOT make generic statements — every claim must cite a metric or content excerpt. 10. Scores must be integers from 1 to 10. ## Recommendation Constraints 11. Generate 5 recommendations in the "recommendations" array. Only reduce to 4 if the page is near-perfect in one category, or 3 if the page excels in multiple areas. Default to 5. 12. Every recommendation MUST be directly grounded in your analysis findings — never generic advice. 13. Prioritize by impact: priority 1 = highest impact, must-fix; priority 2 = significant improvement; priority 3 = nice-to-have optimization. 14. The "grounded_metric" field must cite the exact data point (e.g., "4 images missing alt text", "Meta title is 78 chars — exceeds 60-char ideal"). 15. The "action" field must be a specific, implementable step — not vague advice. 16. The "expected_impact" field must describe the concrete expected outcome. 17. Focus recommendations on the lowest-scoring categories first. ## Quality Guards 18. THINK BEFORE SCORING: For each category, first identify the relevant metrics, then compare against best practices, then assess real-world impact — only then assign a score. Never pick a score first and justify it after. 19. ANTI-HALLUCINATION: If the extracted metrics say a feature is MISSING (e.g., "Structured Data (JSON-LD): None" or "Canonical URL: MISSING"), treat it as definitively absent. Do not infer its presence from the page text. 20. NO DUPLICATE RECOMMENDATIONS: Each of the 5 recommendations must address a distinct issue. Do not suggest the same fix in different wording. 21. PROFESSIONAL TONE: Avoid vague marketing buzzwords ("leverage", "supercharge", "synergize"). Write like a senior consultant delivering a client audit report. - 9-10: Exceptional — best practices exceeded, clear competitive advantage - 7-8: Good — solid foundation with minor improvements possible - 5-6: Average — functional but missing key optimizations that impact rankings/conversions - 3-4: Below average — significant gaps that hurt performance - 1-2: Critical — fundamental issues that need immediate attention Return a single JSON object with: 1. Scores and detailed analysis for each of the 5 categories (structure, messaging, CTAs, content depth, UX) 2. A "recommendations" array with 5 prioritized, actionable recommendations grounded in your analysis (reduce to 4 or 3 only if the page truly excels) All in one structured response matching the provided schema. ## Good vs. Bad — calibrate your output quality BAD finding (vague, no data): "The content is too short and doesn't explain the product well." GOOD finding (grounded, specific): "Word count of 300 is below the 600-word threshold for competitive mid-funnel queries, and with no rich media alternatives (0 videos, 0 canvas elements), the page lacks depth to rank." BAD recommendation (generic): "Improve SEO by adding alt text to images." GOOD recommendation (actionable, cited): "Add descriptive alt text to the 4 images missing it (80% of total). Prioritize the hero banner and product thumbnails, as these appear in Google Image search results."
user_prompt: ## Extracted Page Metrics (Deterministic — scraped from HTML) ### Content Metrics - Word Count: 384 - CTA Count: 25 - Internal Links: 49 - External Links: 2 ### Image & Visual Media - Total Images (): 53 - Images Missing Alt Text: 0 (0.0%) - Images with Decorative Alt (alt=""): 51 - Rich Visual Media: Video embeds detected, CSS animations/transitions detected ### Technical SEO - Meta Title: B2B Digital Agency for Enterprises | eight25 (44 chars — ideal: 50-60) - Meta Description: Global B2B digital agency offering enterprise web design, mobile apps, and strategic consulting for Fortune 500 firms. (118 chars — ideal: 120-160) - Viewport Meta: Present - Canonical URL: Present - Robots Meta: Present - Open Graph Tags: Present - Twitter Card: Present - Structured Data (JSON-LD): None ### Heading Structure - Heading Counts: {"h1": 1, "h2": 5, "h3": 10, "h4": 0, "h5": 0, "h6": 0} ## Heading Hierarchy (order as found in HTML) [["H1", "Digital experiences for the modern enterprise"], ["H2", "The world\u2019s best companies"], ["H2", "choose to work with eight25"], ["H3", "Google"], ["H3", "Qlik"], ["H3", "Intralinks"], ["H3", "Andela"], ["H3", "Qualcomm"], ["H2", "Insights"], ["H3", "Analyzing the \u2018Request a Demo\u2019 Page: Unpacking Why Conversions Are Lagging"], ["H3", "Uncovering Homepage Hero Friction: Why Visitors Bounce and How to Fix It"], ["H3", "Why Is Brand Alignment Important For B2B Enterprise Websites?"], ["H3", "Assessment:\u00a0 Is your website working hard enough?"]] ## Page Content (visible text excerpt) Digital experiences for the modern enterprise We build brands and web experiences that help you build the ultimate customer journey. Learn More The world\u2019s best companies choose to work with eight25 Google Qlik Intralinks Andela Qualcomm Insights Analyzing the \u2018Request a Demo\u2019 Page: Unpacking Why Conversions Are Lagging Read More Uncovering Homepage Hero Friction: Why Visitors Bounce and How to Fix It Read More Why Is Brand Alignment Important For B2B Enterprise Websites? Read More Assessment:\u00a0 Is your website working hard enough? We\u2019ve been privileged to work with Fortune 500 companies and funded startups alike, to build some of the most innovative solutions on the web. Review an in-depth assessment analyzing the strengths, weaknesses, and opportunities of your digital presence. Technology Cras mattis consectetur purus sit amet fermentum. Nullam quis risus eget urna mollis ornare vel eu leo. Curabitur blandit tempus porttitor. Growth Cras mattis consectetur purus sit amet fermentum. Nullam quis risus eget urna mollis ornare vel eu leo. Curabitur blandit tempus porttitor. Success stories\u2026 Let\u2019s talk Name Email Phone Message Submit Perform a complete website audit in two parts: **STEP 0 — Page Classification**: First, determine the primary purpose of this page from its content, CTAs, and structure (e.g., SaaS landing page, e-commerce product page, blog/article, documentation, portfolio, corporate homepage). Tailor your scoring expectations to that page type — a blog post needs fewer CTAs than a landing page, and a portfolio site may rely on visuals over word count. **PART 1 — Analysis**: Evaluate the page across all five categories: - **Structure & SEO**: Heading hierarchy (H1 presence, proper nesting, no skips), meta tag quality (title/description length and relevance), technical SEO signals (viewport, canonical, OG tags, structured data), and overall HTML organization. - **Messaging & Clarity**: Value proposition clarity, brand voice consistency, whether the content answers the user's likely intent, and if key messages are prioritized above the fold. - **CTAs**: Quantity relative to page length, action-oriented language quality, variety of CTAs (primary vs secondary), and strategic placement signals. - **Content Depth**: Word count relative to topic complexity, use of subheadings for scannability, internal/external link strategy for topical authority, and whether the content demonstrates expertise (E-E-A-T signals). - **UX**: Content readability structure (short paragraphs, logical flow), mobile-readiness signals (viewport meta), image optimization (alt text), rich media usage for engagement, and overall page organization. For each category, provide a score (1-10), findings grounded in specific metrics, and direct evidence. **PART 2 — Recommendations**: Based on your analysis above, generate 5 prioritized recommendations (reduce to 4 or 3 only if the page truly excels in most categories). Focus on the lowest-scoring categories first. Each recommendation must cite specific data and be the kind a web agency would include in a client audit report. Return everything as a single structured JSON object matching the schema.

structure_analysis.findings: The page has a single H1, and the heading hierarchy (H2s and H3s) is generally well-structured without obvious skips. However, the meta title (44 chars) is too short, falling outside the ideal 50-60 character range, and the meta description (118 chars) is slightly short of the ideal 120-160 character range. While many technical SEO signals are present (Viewport, Canonical, OG, Twitter Card), the absence of Structured Data (JSON-LD) is a missed opportunity for rich snippets and improved search visibility. 51 out of 53 images (96%) have decorative alt text, which is not ideal for SEO or accessibility.
structure_analysis.evidence: Heading Counts: {"h1": 1, "h2": 5, "h3": 10, "h4": 0, "h5": 0, "h6": 0}. Meta Title: B2B Digital Agency for Enterprises | eight25 (44 chars — ideal: 50-60). Meta Description: Global B2B digital agency offering enterprise web design, mobile apps, and strategic consulting for Fortune 500 firms. (118 chars — ideal: 120-160). Structured Data (JSON-LD): None. Images with Decorative Alt (alt=""): 51.

messaging_analysis.findings: The primary value proposition, 'Digital experiences for the modern enterprise,' is clear and prominently displayed. The messaging aligns with the target audience (enterprises, Fortune 500 firms) and service offerings (web design, mobile apps, consulting). The content effectively communicates the agency's capabilities and client successes. However, the 'Studio Services' section has placeholder text ('Cras mattis consectetur purus sit amet fermentum. Nullam quis risus eget urna mollis ornare vel eu leo. Curabitur blandit tempus porttitor.') which detracts from clarity and professionalism.

cta_analysis.findings: The page features a robust 25 CTAs, which is appropriate for a corporate homepage aiming to guide users through various services and contact points. CTAs like 'Learn More', 'Read More', 'Let's talk', and 'Submit' are present and action-oriented. Multiple CTAs are visible above the fold, including 'Learn More' and 'Let's talk'. The variety includes service-specific links, blog post links, and a primary contact form.
cta_analysis.evidence: CTA Count: 25. CTAs visible above fold: 'Learn More', 'Let's talk'. Page Content Excerpt: 'Learn More', 'Read More', 'Let's talk', 'Submit'.

content_depth_analysis.findings: The word count of 384 is on the lower side for a comprehensive corporate homepage aiming to cover a wide range of services and demonstrate expertise. While there are 5 H2s and 10 H3s, which helps break up content, the topical coverage feels somewhat superficial given the breadth of services offered. The page includes 49 internal links, which is good for site navigation and SEO, but only 2 external links. The presence of placeholder text in the 'Studio Services' section significantly undermines the demonstration of expertise and topical depth.
content_depth_analysis.evidence: Word Count: 384. Heading Counts: {"h2": 5, "h3": 10}. Internal Links: 49. External Links: 2. Page Content Excerpt: 'Cras mattis consectetur purus sit amet fermentum. Nullam quis risus eget urna mollis ornare vel eu leo. Curabitur blandit tempus porttitor.'

ux_analysis.findings: The page utilizes rich media, including video embeds and CSS animations, which enhance user engagement. The presence of a viewport meta tag indicates mobile-readiness. All images have alt text, though 96% are decorative, which is a missed SEO and accessibility opportunity. The heading structure aids scannability. However, the placeholder text in the 'Studio Services' section creates a poor user experience and detracts from the overall professionalism and readability.

recommendations.title: Implement Structured Data (JSON-LD)
recommendations.description: The page is missing structured data (JSON-LD), which is crucial for search engines to understand the page content and can lead to rich snippets in search results, improving click-through rates. This is a fundamental SEO signal for corporate pages.
recommendations.action: Implement JSON-LD schema markup for the organization, services, and potentially blog posts or case studies featured on the page.
recommendations.expected_impact: Improved visibility in search results through rich snippets, enhanced understanding of page content by search engines, and potentially higher click-through rates.

recommendations.title: Replace Placeholder Text and Expand Service Descriptions
recommendations.description: The 'Studio Services' section contains placeholder text ('Cras mattis consectetur purus sit amet fermentum...'), which is unprofessional and provides no value to the user or search engines. The overall word count of 384 is also relatively low for a page detailing a broad range of enterprise services.
recommendations.grounded_metric: Page Content Excerpt: 'Cras mattis consectetur purus sit amet fermentum. Nullam quis risus eget urna mollis ornare vel eu leo. Curabitur blandit tempus porttitor.' and Word Count: 384
recommendations.action: Replace the placeholder text with detailed descriptions of the studio services offered. Expand on other service sections to increase the overall word count and provide more depth.
recommendations.expected_impact: Improved user understanding of services, enhanced E-E-A-T signals, better topical coverage for relevant keywords, and a more professional user experience.

recommendations.title: Optimize Meta Title and Description Lengths
recommendations.description: The meta title is too short (44 chars) and the meta description is slightly short (118 chars) of the ideal lengths (50-60 and 120-160 chars respectively). This can impact click-through rates from search results and how well the page's purpose is communicated.
recommendations.grounded_metric: Meta Title: B2B Digital Agency for Enterprises | eight25 (44 chars — ideal: 50-60). Meta Description: Global B2B digital agency offering enterprise web design, mobile apps, and strategic consulting for Fortune 500 firms. (118 chars — ideal: 120-160)
recommendations.action: Expand the meta title to be between 50-60 characters and the meta description to be between 120-160 characters, ensuring they accurately reflect the page's content and target keywords.
recommendations.expected_impact: Improved search result appearance, potentially higher click-through rates from organic search, and clearer communication of page relevance to users and search engines.

recommendations.title: Enhance Image Alt Text for SEO and Accessibility
recommendations.description: While all images have alt text, 96% (51 out of 53) are marked as decorative. This means they are not providing descriptive context for search engines or users with screen readers, missing an opportunity to improve SEO and accessibility.
recommendations.action: Review the 51 images currently marked as decorative and add descriptive alt text that accurately reflects the image content and its relevance to the page, especially for images that visually represent services or client work.
recommendations.expected_impact: Improved image search visibility, enhanced accessibility for users with disabilities, and better contextual understanding of the page content by search engines.

recommendations.title: Increase External Linking Strategy
recommendations.description: The page has a low number of external links (2) relative to its content depth and breadth. Linking to authoritative external resources can help establish topical authority and provide additional value to users.
recommendations.action: Identify relevant, authoritative external resources (e.g., industry reports, reputable publications, partner sites) and link to them contextually within the content.
recommendations.expected_impact: Strengthened topical authority, improved credibility by referencing external expertise, and enhanced user experience by providing further resources.
